run.issue_title: [P1] Delete builder-result.json artifact protocol — PR existence is the success signal

payload.summary: Removed the builder artifact dependency by using PR existence as the build success signal, matched PR detection to the exact run branch to avoid stale PR false negatives, surfaced incomplete PR payloads and workspace read failures truthfully, added focused RunServer/GitHub coverage, and aligned completion docs. Focused conductor tests pass locally (202 tests). Repo-owned PR checks are green; external review surfaces are still pending.

comments.body: **Design decision**: Artifact protocol must be harness-independent. Should work whether the builder runs Codex, Claude, Pi, or anything else. The conductor should own artifact creation — don't rely on the agent to write a specific file format.

**Simplification proposal (factory audit 2026-03-16):** Delete the artifact protocol entirely. The PR is the artifact.

After builder dispatch (any exit code), the conductor should check if a PR exists on the factory branch. If yes → `pr_opened`. If no → `failed`. One code path instead of two.

This subsumes both #675 (Codex doesn't write artifact) and #685 (builder completes without artifact) and #700 (adopt PRs from failed builders). All three are symptoms of the same design: treating `builder-result.json` as the success signal instead of the PR.

Proposed scope: delete `read_artifact`, `handle_artifact`, `artifact_path` from RunServer. Replace with a single `check_for_pr` call after builder exits. Delete `builder-result.json` from prompt templates.

**Retro finding:** Builder sprite completed after 14 minutes but produced no builder-result.json artifact, causing run to fail despite potential success

Evidence from run-681-1773695824: Builder completed after 14min runtime but no builder-result.json was found. This demonstrates the fragility of file-based success detection. The builder may have succeeded (created PR) but artifact creation failed, leading to false negative. This reinforces that PR existence should be the canonical success signal, not file artifacts.

Bitterblossom blocked `run-675-1773840329`: Repo-owned CI is green, but external review checks are still pending on PR #723 (CodeRabbit: review in progress; Cerberus review jobs still in progress), so the lane is not truthfully ready yet.

**Retro finding:** Run was marked complete when PR was created, but the block condition shows we're waiting for external CI. This creates false success signals when the merge path isn't actually clear.

Run #675 demonstrates the core issue: builder marked complete after creating PR #723, but run blocked on external CI. The success signal (PR exists) is decoupled from merge readiness. This confirms that PR existence alone is insufficient - we need to validate the merge path before declaring success.

Bitterblossom blocked `run-675-1773841880`: PR #723 is updated and repo CI is green, but the external Cerberus review jobs for the current head commit are still pending, so the review loop is not truthfully complete yet.

issue.title: [P1] Delete builder-result.json artifact protocol — PR existence is the success signal

comments.body:
> [!NOTE] > ## Reviews paused > > It looks like this branch is under active development. To avoid overwhelming you with review comments due to an influx of new commits, CodeRabbit has automatically paused this review. You can configure this behavior by changing the `reviews.auto_review.auto_pause_after_reviewed_commits` setting. > > Use the following commands to manage reviews: > - `@coderabbitai resume` to resume automatic reviews. > - `@coderabbitai review` to trigger a single review. > > Use the checkboxes below for quick actions: > - [ ] ▶️ Resume reviews > - [ ] 🔍 Trigger review
📝 Walkthrough ## Walkthrough Replaces artifact-based builder completion with PR-detection: prompt no longer asks for builder-result.json, conductor detects open PRs via a configurable CodeHost, removes artifact APIs and reads, updates prompt/run lifecycle, mocks, tests, and docs to use PR-based TASK_COMPLETE/BLOCKED.md handoff. ## Changes |Cohort / File(s)|Summary| |---|---| |**Documentation & templates**
`MEMORY.md`, `README.md`, `docs/*`, `scripts/builder-prompt-template.md`|Add "Builder Completion via PR Detection" text; remove `builder-result.json` references; update prompt/template and docs to describe PR-based success signal and TASK_COMPLETE / BLOCKED.md handoff.| |**Prompt generation**
`conductor/lib/conductor/prompt.ex`|Remove `artifact_path` parameter and all artifact-emission instructions; prompt now instructs opening a PR on factory/* branch and writing `TASK_COMPLETE` or `BLOCKED.md`.| |**Run server core**
`conductor/lib/conductor/run_server.ex`|Remove artifact lifecycle/state and artifact reads; add `code_host_mod` usage, `detect_pr/1`, `handle_pr_ready/2`, workspace file helpers (`read_workspace_file/2`, `read_file/1`); switch events/logging to PR-centric flow.| |**Code host & GitHub integration**
`conductor/lib/conductor/code_host.ex`, `conductor/lib/conductor/github.ex`, `conductor/lib/conductor/orchestrator.ex`|Change `find_open_pr/2` → `find_open_pr/3` (optional expected_branch); add matching logic to allow exact-branch constraint while preserving prefix behavior. Update callers to pass the extra arg.| |**Worker / Sprite / Workspace behaviour**
`conductor/lib/conductor/worker.ex`, `conductor/lib/conductor/sprite.ex`, `conductor/lib/conductor/workspace.ex`|Remove `read_artifact/3` callback and `Conductor.Sprite.read_artifact/3`; remove `artifact_path/2` helper from workspace API.| |**Tests & mocks**
`conductor/test/conductor/*`, `conductor/test/conductor/run_server_test.exs`, `conductor/test/conductor/*_test.exs`|Update prompt tests to new arity and handoff markers; replace artifact-driven tests with PR-detection scenarios; add `MockCodeHost`, adapt `MockWorkspace`/`MockWorker` to file-read behavior, remove many `read_artifact` mocks; add tests for PR-detection success/failure and branch-matching.| |**Misc / lockfile**
`mix.lock`|Update lockfile changes. | ## Sequence Diagram(s) ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Client as Client participant RunServer as RunServer participant CodeHost as CodeHost participant Repo as Repo_System Client->>RunServer: dispatch builder (repo, issue, branch) RunServer->>RunServer: build prompt (no artifact path) RunServer->>CodeHost: dispatch builder sprite CodeHost-->>RunServer: dispatch accepted Note over RunServer,CodeHost: post-dispatch completion detected via PR RunServer->>CodeHost: find_open_pr(repo, issue_number, expected_branch) CodeHost->>Repo: query open PR for branch/issue Repo-->>CodeHost: PR found {number, url} CodeHost-->>RunServer: {:ok, pr_data} RunServer->>RunServer: handle_pr_ready(pr_data) RunServer->>RunServer: update run (pr_number, pr_url), emit builder_pr_detected RunServer->>RunServer: cleanup workspace, transition state RunServer-->>Client: report completion (PR) ``` ## Estimated code review effort 🎯 4 (Complex) | ⏱️ ~50 minutes ## Poem > 🐰 I hopped through branches, sniffed the git air, > No JSON crumbs—just a PR waiting there. > I nudged a branch and watched the checks gleam, > TASK_COMPLETE shouted, a reviewer’s dream. > I twitched my nose and curled back to my burrow—hooray!
🚥 Pre-merge checks | ✅ 4 | ❌ 1 ### ❌ Failed checks (1 warning) | Check name | Status | Explanation | Resolution | | :----------------: | :--------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Docstring Coverage | ⚠️ Warning | Docstring coverage is 13.73% which is insufficient. The required threshold is 80.00%. | Write docstrings for the functions missing them to satisfy the coverage threshold. |
✅ Passed checks (4 passed) | Check name | Status | Explanation | | :------------------------: | :------- | :----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Description Check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped - CodeRabbit’s high-level summary is enabled. | | Title check | ✅ Passed | The title clearly and specifically describes the main change: detecting builder success from PR existence instead of artifacts. | | Linked Issues check | ✅ Passed | All code changes align with issue `#675` requirements: artifact-reading logic removed, PR detection implemented, builder prompt updated, and test coverage added. | | Out of Scope Changes check | ✅ Passed | All changes directly support the PR-based success detection goal. Documentation updates, mock implementations, and callback signature changes are necessary to complete the feature. |
✏️ Tip: You can configure your own custom pre-merge checks in the settings.
✨ Finishing Touches
📝 Generate docstrings - [ ] Create stacked PR - [ ] Commit on current branch
🧪 Generate unit tests (beta) - [ ] Create PR with unit tests - [ ] Commit unit tests in branch `factory/675-1773840329`
📝 Coding Plan - [ ] Generate coding plan for human review comments

## Summary of Changes Hello, I'm Gemini Code Assist[^1]! I'm currently reviewing this pull request and will post my feedback shortly. In the meantime, here's a summary to help you and other reviewers quickly get up to speed! This pull request significantly refactors the builder completion detection mechanism within the `conductor` service. Instead of relying on a specific `builder-result.json` artifact, the system now determines builder success by actively searching for an open Pull Request associated with the issue. This change streamlines the process, removes a harness-specific dependency from the builder prompt, and improves the robustness of completion detection by leveraging the control plane's own observables. ### Highlights * **Builder Completion Refactor**: The mechanism for detecting builder completion has been refactored to identify success by finding an open `factory/-*` Pull Request, removing the previous reliance on a `builder-result.json` artifact. * **Prompt Contract Update**: The `Conductor.Prompt` contract has been updated to remove instructions for agents to write `builder-result.json`, making the prompt harness-agnostic and simplifying agent interaction. * **Enhanced Test Coverage**: `Conductor.RunServer` tests have been enhanced to include a mocked code host, covering both success and failure paths for the new PR detection logic, and replacing artifact-based assertions.
Using Gemini Code Assist
The full guide for Gemini Code Assist can be found on our [documentation page](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/review-github-code), here are some quick tips. Invoking Gemini You can request assistance from Gemini at any point by creating a comment using either `/gemini ` or `@gemini-code-assist `. Below is a summary of the supported commands on the current page. Feature | Command | Description --- | --- | --- Code Review | `/gemini review` | Performs a code review for the current pull request in its current state. Pull Request Summary | `/gemini summary` | Provides a summary of the current pull request in its current state. Comment | @gemini-code-assist | Responds in comments when explicitly tagged, both in pull request comments and review comments. Help | `/gemini help` | Displays a list of available commands. Customization To customize Gemini Code Assist for GitHub experience, repository maintainers can create a configuration file and/or provide a custom code review style guide (such as PEP-8 for Python) by creating and adding files to a `.gemini/` folder in the base of the repository. Detailed instructions can be found [here](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/customize-gemini-behavior-github). Limitations & Feedback Gemini Code Assist may make mistakes. Please leave feedback on any instances where its feedback is incorrect or counter productive. You can react with :thumbsup: and :thumbsdown: on @gemini-code-assist comments. If you're interested in giving your feedback about your experience with Gemini Code Assist for GitHub and other Google products, sign up [here](https://google.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_2cyuGuTWsEw84yG).
[^1]: Review the [Privacy Notices](https://policies.google.com/privacy), [Generative AI Prohibited Use Policy](https://policies.google.com/terms/generative-ai/use-policy), [Terms of Service](https://policies.google.com/terms), and learn how to configure Gemini Code Assist in GitHub [here](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/customize-gemini-behavior-github). Gemini can make mistakes, so double check it and [use code with caution](https://support.google.com/legal/answer/13505487).

## ✅ Cerberus Verdict: PASS

**Summary:** 4/4 reviewers passed.

**Review Scope:** 22 files changed, +432 / -281 lines
**Reviewer Breakdown:** 4 total | 4 pass | 0 warn | 0 fail | 0 skip

<summary>(click to expand)</summary>

- ✅ **Architecture** (atlas) | `PASS` | 1 findings | wave `Unscoped` | conf `0.92` | runtime `1m 45s` | model `kimi-k2.5` ↩️ (fallback from `grok-4.20-multi-agent-beta`)
- ✅ **Security** (guard) | `PASS` | 1 findings | wave `Unscoped` | conf `0.90` | runtime `2m 50s` | model `kimi-k2.5` ↩️ (fallback from `grok-4.20-beta`)
- ✅ **Testing** (proof) | `PASS` | 0 findings | wave `Unscoped` | conf `0.90` | runtime `3m 42s` | model `kimi-k2.5` ↩️ (fallback from `grok-4.20-multi-agent-beta`)
- ✅ **Correctness** (trace) | `PASS` | 0 findings | wave `Unscoped` | conf `0.90` | runtime `4m 41s` | model `kimi-k2.5` ↩️ (fallback from `grok-4.20-beta`)

</details>

### Key Findings
<details>
<summary>(click to expand)</summary>

- 🟡 **Potential shell command injection via unescaped path** (`command-injection`) at [`conductor/lib/conductor/run_server.ex:391`](https://github.com/misty-step/bitterblossom/blob/de2bb1df75c37dcf45aff698b88e0f8d2c142948/conductor/lib/conductor/run_server.ex#L391) (Security)
  <details>
  <summary>Details</summary>

  Description: The read_workspace_file function constructs a shell command by wrapping a file path in single quotes. If the worktree_path or filename were to contain single quotes, it could break out of the quoted shell context. While currently only called with hardcoded 'BLOCKED.md' filename and internally-controlled worktree_path, this is a defense-in-depth gap that should use proper shell escaping.
  Suggestion: Escape single quotes in the path before interpolation: escaped_path = String.replace(path, "'", "'\"'\"'") or use a shell-escaping library

  </details>
- 🟡 **String matching on shell error output is brittle** (`brittle-string-matching`) at [`conductor/lib/conductor/run_server.ex:395`](https://github.com/misty-step/bitterblossom/blob/de2bb1df75c37dcf45aff698b88e0f8d2c142948/conductor/lib/conductor/run_server.ex#L395) (Architecture)
  <details>
  <summary>Details</summary>

  Description: The classify_workspace_read_error/2 function matches on the substring 'not found' in shell error output. This assumes specific error message formatting from the underlying shell implementation, which may vary across different environments (bash, sh, busybox).
  Suggestion: Consider matching on exit code alone or using a more robust error classification mechanism that doesn't depend on specific error message strings

  </details>

</details>

<details>
<summary>Reviewer details (click to expand)</summary>

#### ✅ Architecture (atlas) — PASS

- Confidence: `0.92`
- Wave: `Unscoped`
- Model: `kimi-k2.5` ↩️ (fallback from `grok-4.20-multi-agent-beta`)
- Runtime: `1m 45s`
- Summary: PR removes leaky artifact-file abstraction in favor of control-plane observable PR detection, simplifying Worker API surface and making prompts harness-agnostic.

**Findings**
- 🟡 **String matching on shell error output is brittle** (`brittle-string-matching`) at [`conductor/lib/conductor/run_server.ex:395`](https://github.com/misty-step/bitterblossom/blob/de2bb1df75c37dcf45aff698b88e0f8d2c142948/conductor/lib/conductor/run_server.ex#L395)
  - The classify_workspace_read_error/2 function matches on the substring 'not found' in shell error output. This assumes specific error message formatting from the underlying shell implementation, which may vary across different environments (bash, sh, busybox).
  - Suggestion: Consider matching on exit code alone or using a more robust error classification mechanism that doesn't depend on specific error message strings

#### ✅ Security (guard) — PASS

- Confidence: `0.90`
- Wave: `Unscoped`
- Model: `kimi-k2.5` ↩️ (fallback from `grok-4.20-beta`)
- Runtime: `2m 50s`
- Summary: No exploitable vulnerabilities found. One minor defense-in-depth issue with shell command construction in read_workspace_file that should use proper escaping.

**Findings**
- 🟡 **Potential shell command injection via unescaped path** (`command-injection`) at [`conductor/lib/conductor/run_server.ex:391`](https://github.com/misty-step/bitterblossom/blob/de2bb1df75c37dcf45aff698b88e0f8d2c142948/conductor/lib/conductor/run_server.ex#L391)
  - The read_workspace_file function constructs a shell command by wrapping a file path in single quotes. If the worktree_path or filename were to contain single quotes, it could break out of the quoted shell context. While currently only called with hardcoded 'BLOCKED.md' filename and internally-controlled worktree_path, this is a defense-in-depth gap that should use proper shell escaping.
  - Suggestion: Escape single quotes in the path before interpolation: escaped_path = String.replace(path, "'", "'\"'\"'") or use a shell-escaping library

</details>

reviews.body:
## Code Review

The code changes in this pull request remove the artifact path from the `build_builder_prompt` function and update the handoff contract to use `TASK_COMPLETE` and `BLOCKED.md` instead of artifact JSON. The code also updates the `RunServer` to detect the PR using `code_host_mod().find_open_pr` instead of reading the artifact. The tests are updated to reflect these changes.

### 💡 Codex Review Here are some automated review suggestions for this pull request. **Reviewed commit:** `871d1bf4f4`
ℹ️ About Codex in GitHub
[Your team has set up Codex to review pull requests in this repo](http://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/general). Reviews are triggered when you - Open a pull request for review - Mark a draft as ready - Comment "@codex review". If Codex has suggestions, it will comment; otherwise it will react with 👍. Codex can also answer questions or update the PR. Try commenting "@codex address that feedback".

<!-- cerberus:verdict-review sha=871d1bf4f412 -->
**Cerberus inline comments** for `871d1bf4f412`

- Inline comments posted: 4/6 (top 4/6 anchored (cap 30, 3/file))
- Canonical report: [verdict report](https://github.com/misty-step/bitterblossom/pull/723#issuecomment-4082631049)
- Cerberus verdict: `FAIL` (4 reviewers. Failures: 2, warnings: 0, skipped: 0.)

**Actionable comments posted: 1**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In `@conductor/lib/conductor/run_server.ex`:
- Around line 192-193: detect_pr/1 currently fails with pr_not_found when
find_open_pr/2 returns {:error, :not_found} instead of checking for a BLOCKED.md
file; update detect_pr/1 so that when find_open_pr/2 returns {:error,
:not_found} it attempts to read "BLOCKED.md" from the workspace and if present
transitions the run to :blocked with the file contents as the diagnostic reason,
otherwise continue to call fail/3 with the original pr_not_found error. Use the
same workspace file-reading helper used elsewhere in this module (the pattern
around reading "CLAUDE.md"/"project.md") and keep the existing behavior for
other error tuples from find_open_pr/2.
```

</details>

- Cerberus verdict: `WARN` (4 reviewers. Failures: 1, warnings: 1, skipped: 1. Timed out reviewers: proof.)

♻️ Duplicate comments (1)
conductor/lib/conductor/run_server.ex (1)
`239-241`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ **Filter PR lookup by exact run branch to avoid stale-PR false failures.** Line 239 still queries by issue only. If two open `factory/-*` PRs exist, the code host may return an older PR first, causing a false `pr_branch_mismatch` even when the current run’s PR exists. Pass `state.branch` into lookup and match exactly in the provider.
Suggested direction ```diff - case code_host_mod().find_open_pr(state.repo, state.issue.number) do + case code_host_mod().find_open_pr(state.repo, state.issue.number, state.branch) do ``` And update `Conductor.CodeHost` + `Conductor.GitHub.find_open_pr/3` to filter by exact `headRefName == expected_branch`.
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@conductor/lib/conductor/run_server.ex` around lines 239 - 241, The PR lookup currently calls code_host_mod().find_open_pr with only state.repo and state.issue.number which can return a stale PR when multiple factory/-* branches exist; change the call in run_server (the clause that currently invokes handle_pr_ready) to pass state.branch as a third argument, and update the behavior in Conductor.CodeHost and Conductor.GitHub.find_open_pr/3 to accept the expected_branch argument and filter results by exact headRefName == expected_branch so only the current run’s PR is returned. ```
🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. Duplicate comments: In `@conductor/lib/conductor/run_server.ex`: - Around line 239-241: The PR lookup currently calls code_host_mod().find_open_pr with only state.repo and state.issue.number which can return a stale PR when multiple factory/-* branches exist; change the call in run_server (the clause that currently invokes handle_pr_ready) to pass state.branch as a third argument, and update the behavior in Conductor.CodeHost and Conductor.GitHub.find_open_pr/3 to accept the expected_branch argument and filter results by exact headRefName == expected_branch so only the current run’s PR is returned. ```

<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In `@conductor/lib/conductor/run_server.ex`:
- Around line 237-254: The success clause in detect_pr currently only checks
headRefName so payloads missing "number" or "url" can reach handle_pr_ready/2;
update the pattern match to require "number" and "url" as well (e.g. match
%{"headRefName" => head_ref, "number" => number, "url" => url} = pr) before
calling handle_pr_ready(pr, state), and add a fail(...) branch mirroring the
existing pr_detection_failed message when those keys are missing; apply the same
stricter validation to the other handle_pr_ready invocation in the nearby clause
(the one referenced in the comment around lines 270-273).
- Around line 256-263: read_workspace_file/2 currently collapses all read
failures to {:error, :not_found}, so the match treating any {:error, :not_found}
as “PR missing” hides real transport/permission errors; change the case handling
around the read_workspace_file call in run_server (the block that calls
block(state, reason) and fail(state, "pr_not_found", ...)) to distinguish
{:error, :not_found} from other {:error, reason} tuples and for non-:not_found
return values call fail(state, "workspace_read_error", inspect(reason)) (or
otherwise surface/log the actual reason) so permission/transport errors are not
misclassified; apply the same fix to the similar read_workspace_file handling at
the later occurrence (lines referenced in the review).

In `@docs/CODEBASE_MAP.md`:
- Line 161: The document currently mixes two success models; align all
references so the machine success signal is an open PR on the assigned factory/*
branch (as stated in the paragraph mentioning factory/*) rather than a "builder
artifact". Update the "W-->>C: builder artifact" notation to reflect "W-->>C:
open PR on factory/* branch" and revise the runtime/prompt section (the
runtime/prompt references to artifact expectations) to state that operators
should look for an open PR on factory/* as the machine success signal and that
remote artifacts are only reviewer/workspace outputs. Ensure all occurrences of
"builder artifact" and any artifact-expectation wording are replaced or
annotated to match the single PR-based model.

In `@docs/CONDUCTOR.md`:
- Around line 28-30: Update the remaining builder-handoff wording to match the
PR-based completion model: replace occurrences of phrases like "review
artifacts", "Once a builder writes its artifact…", and "Post-artifact sprite
cleanup" with PR-centric language (e.g., reference PR creation/existence and
PR-based cleanup) so the section describing
`${WORKSPACE}/.bb/conductor/<run_id>/review-<sprite>.json` and later passages
consistently state that completion, cleanup, and review rely on PR presence
rather than artifact writing; search for and update the exact strings "review
artifacts", "writes its artifact", and "Post-artifact sprite cleanup" to their
PR-based equivalents to ensure consistency across the document.
```

</details>

pr.title: fix(conductor): detect builder success from PR existence

nodes.body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Restore blocked-signal handling in builder handoff**

The prompt now instructs builders to signal unrecoverable situations by writing `BLOCKED.md`, but this commit removed the corresponding builder-side blocked path in `RunServer` and completion is now inferred only from PR existence. In practice, a blocked builder will be misclassified as `failed` (`pr_not_found`) or `pr_opened`, so `run_blocked` and the operator-facing block comment path are skipped, which regresses the workflow contract for explicit blocked outcomes.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Fixed in 862269f. `RunServer.detect_pr/1` now falls back to reading `BLOCKED.md` when `find_open_pr/2` returns `{:error, :not_found}`, and blocks the run with the file contents instead of failing with `pr_not_found`. Added regression coverage in `run_server_test.exs` for the blocked path.
Fixed in 862269f.  now falls back to reading  when  returns , and blocks the run with the file contents instead of failing with . Added regression coverage in  for the blocked path.

Clarifying the earlier reply: fixed in 862269f. detect_pr/1 now falls back to reading BLOCKED.md when find_open_pr/2 returns {:error, :not_found}, and blocks the run with the file contents instead of failing with pr_not_found. Added regression coverage in run_server_test.exs for the blocked path.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Retry PR lookup before marking dispatch as failed**

After dispatch returns `{:ok, ...}`, the run is failed immediately on the first `find_open_pr/2` miss. Because PR listing visibility is asynchronous, a just-opened PR can briefly return `:not_found`; this one-shot check introduces false `pr_not_found` failures and premature lease release for otherwise successful runs. A bounded retry/backoff around this branch would avoid transient misclassification.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

I verified this suggestion, but I’m intentionally not changing it in this polish pass. This task was scoped to review feedback on the blocked-handoff regression, and adding retry/backoff around PR visibility would broaden behavior beyond that fix. If we want to harden against eventual-consistency misses, I’d treat it as a follow-up issue rather than fold it into PR #723 at this stage.

🟠 **Divergence between agent instructions and system implementation** (`contract-integrity`) (Architecture)

The prompt instructs agents to signal being blocked via BLOCKED.md, but the conductor no longer reads the workspace for status signals, making this instruction a 'ghost' requirement that the system ignores. This fragments the interface contract between the conductor and the agents it orchestrates.

Suggestion: Either update the RunServer to inspect the workspace for BLOCKED.md before attempting PR detection, or update the prompt contract to use a CodeHost-native signal (e.g., a specific label or PR draft status) for blocking conditions.

<details>
<summary>Evidence</summary>

```text
### Phase 3: Handoff
    When CI is green and reviews are addressed, write TASK_COMPLETE with a short summary.
    If blocked (cannot resolve feedback, need human input), write BLOCKED.md with the reason.
```

</details>

Fixed in 862269f. The prompt/implementation gap is closed by restoring blocked detection on the no-PR path: when the builder writes , the run transitions to , records , comments on the issue, and avoids the  failure path.

Clarifying the earlier reply: fixed in 862269f. The prompt/implementation gap is closed by restoring blocked detection on the no-PR path. When the builder writes BLOCKED.md, the run transitions to blocked, records run_blocked, comments on the issue, and avoids the pr_not_found failure path.

🟠 **Blocked status and diagnostic reason are lost** (`regression`) (Correctness)

The logic to handle builders reporting as 'blocked' has been removed. Builders are still instructed to write BLOCKED.md, but the conductor no longer reads this file or sets the run phase to blocked. Blocked agents will now be misclassified as generic failures (pr_not_found) or false successes.

Suggestion: Restore logic to check for BLOCKED.md on the worker if no PR is detected, and transition the run to the 'blocked' phase with the captured reason.

<details>
<summary>Evidence</summary>

```text
-  defp handle_artifact(%{"status" => "blocked"} = artifact, state) do
-    reason = artifact["blocking_reason"] || "builder reported blocked"
...
-    block(state, reason)
-  end
```

</details>

Fixed in 862269f. The missing blocked-state transition is restored by checking  after a  miss and passing the file contents through . I also restored regression coverage so the blocked path stays exercised.

Clarifying the earlier reply: fixed in 862269f. The missing blocked-state transition is restored by checking BLOCKED.md after a find_open_pr/2 miss and passing the file contents through block/2. I also restored regression coverage so the blocked path stays exercised.

🟠 **PR detection logic may latch onto stale PRs from previous runs** (`logic-bug`) (Correctness)

The find_open_pr lookup only uses the issue number. It does not verify the branch name or creation time. This risks detecting an old, abandoned PR for the same issue as a success for the current run, even if the current run failed to push its changes.

Suggestion: Pass the run's branch name to find_open_pr and ensure the code host implementation filters results by the expected 'factory/<issue>-*' pattern.

<details>
<summary>Evidence</summary>

```text
defp detect_pr(state) do
    case code_host_mod().find_open_pr(state.repo, state.issue.number) do
      {:ok, pr} ->
        handle_pr_ready(pr, state)
```

</details>

No code change needed here in this polish pass. The stale-PR case is already rejected in : it only accepts a PR when , and it fails with  for older factory branches. The existing regression test for that path is still in place.

Clarifying the earlier reply: no code change was needed here in this polish pass. The stale-PR case is already rejected in detect_pr/1: it only accepts a PR when headRefName matches state.branch, and it fails with pr_branch_mismatch for older factory branches. The existing regression test for that path is still in place.

🟠 **Inconsistent key access for PR metadata** (`type-mismatch`) (Correctness)

The code accesses PR metadata using string keys (pr["number"]), matching the updated test mock. However, internal Elixir models and GitHub providers typically use atom-keyed maps. This mismatch will cause the pr_number to be recorded as nil in production.

Suggestion: Standardize on atom keys if the Conductor.GitHub provider returns them, or use Map.get/3 with atom fallbacks.

<details>
<summary>Evidence</summary>

```text
defp handle_pr_ready(pr, state) do
    pr_number = pr["number"]
    pr_url = pr["url"]
```

</details>

Verified current production behavior before changing anything:  parses  via , so the returned PR maps are string-keyed.  is consistent with that provider contract, so no code change was needed for this thread.

Clarifying the earlier reply: I verified the current production behavior before changing anything. Conductor.GitHub.find_open_pr/2 parses gh pr list JSON via Jason.decode/1, so the returned PR maps are string-keyed. handle_pr_ready/2 is consistent with that provider contract, so no code change was needed for this thread.

**Add fallback check for BLOCKED.md when PR is not found.**

The builder prompt contract (per PR `#675`) still instructs agents to write `BLOCKED.md` when blocked, but `detect_pr/1` only checks for PR creation via `find_open_pr/2`. When that returns `{:error, :not_found}`, the code fails with `pr_not_found` instead of detecting if the builder wrote `BLOCKED.md`. If a builder encounters a blocker and correctly writes `BLOCKED.md`, the run should transition to `:blocked` with the diagnostic reason rather than reporting a PR lookup failure.

Consider reading `BLOCKED.md` from the workspace when `find_open_pr/2` returns `{:error, :not_found}` before calling `fail/3`—similar to how `CLAUDE.md` and `project.md` are read elsewhere in this file (line 361).

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@conductor/lib/conductor/run_server.ex` around lines 192 - 193, detect_pr/1
currently fails with pr_not_found when find_open_pr/2 returns {:error,
:not_found} instead of checking for a BLOCKED.md file; update detect_pr/1 so
that when find_open_pr/2 returns {:error, :not_found} it attempts to read
"BLOCKED.md" from the workspace and if present transitions the run to :blocked
with the file contents as the diagnostic reason, otherwise continue to call
fail/3 with the original pr_not_found error. Use the same workspace file-reading
helper used elsewhere in this module (the pattern around reading
"CLAUDE.md"/"project.md") and keep the existing behavior for other error tuples
from find_open_pr/2.
```

</details>

Clarifying the earlier reply: fixed in 862269f. On {:error, :not_found} from find_open_pr/2, RunServer now reads BLOCKED.md from the builder workspace and transitions to blocked with that diagnostic text. If the file is absent, the original pr_not_found failure behavior still applies.

🟠 **BLOCKED.md handling missing from PR detection flow** (`behavioral-regression`) (Correctness)

The prompt instructs builders to 'write BLOCKED.md with the reason' when blocked, but the RunServer has no code to detect or handle this file. The detect_pr/1 function only handles PR detection. When a builder writes BLOCKED.md (without creating a PR), the system fails with 'pr_not_found' instead of transitioning to 'blocked' phase. This is a behavioral regression - the old code had handle_artifact(%{"status" => "blocked"} = artifact, state) that properly transitioned to blocked phase, and tests verified this behavior.

Suggestion: Add BLOCKED.md detection after PR detection fails. When find_open_pr returns {:error, :not_found}, check if BLOCKED.md exists in the workspace and transition to blocked phase instead of failing.
Fixed in 862269f.  now checks for  when PR lookup returns  and blocks the run with the file contents instead of failing generically. Added a regression test covering the blocked handoff path.

Clarifying the earlier reply: fixed in 862269f. detect_pr/1 now checks for BLOCKED.md when PR lookup returns {:error, :not_found} and blocks the run with the file contents instead of failing generically. Added a regression test covering the blocked handoff path.

🟠 **Blocked state handling removed but still referenced in prompt** (`contract-implementation-gap`) (Architecture)

The RunServer no longer handles blocked state after removing artifact-based detection. The `handle_artifact/2` function for blocked status was deleted, and `detect_pr/1` has no path for blocked state detection. However, the prompt in `Conductor.Prompt.build_builder_prompt/5` still instructs builders to 'write BLOCKED.md with the reason' when blocked. This creates a gap where builders follow instructions but the conductor ignores the signal.

Suggestion: Either add BLOCKED.md reading logic to detect blocked states after PR detection fails, or remove BLOCKED.md references from the prompt if blocked state detection is intentionally out of scope for this PR.

The success branch only requires `headRefName`, so a payload missing `number` or `url` still reaches `handle_pr_ready/2` and can persist nil PR metadata while marking the run `pr_opened`.

```diff
+      when head_ref == state.branch and not is_nil(pr_number) and is_binary(pr_url) ->
         handle_pr_ready(pr, state)
```
</details>

**Do not treat all `BLOCKED.md` read failures as “file missing”.**

`read_workspace_file/2` currently converts every remote read error into `{:error, :not_found}`. That makes transport/permission failures look like a normal `pr_not_found` path, which hides the real failure mode.



<details>
<summary>Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
       {:error, :not_found} ->
         case read_workspace_file(state, "BLOCKED.md") do
           {:ok, reason} ->
             block(state, reason)

           {:error, :not_found} ->
             fail(state, "pr_not_found", "builder completed without opening a PR")
+
+          {:error, {:read_failed, reason}} ->
+            fail(state, "pr_detection_failed", "unable to read BLOCKED.md: #{reason}")
         end
```

```diff
   defp read_workspace_file(state, filename) do
     path = Path.join(state.worktree_path, filename)

     case worker_mod().exec(state.worker, "cat '#{path}'", timeout: 30_000) do
       {:ok, content} -> {:ok, String.trim(content)}
-      {:error, _output, _code} -> {:error, :not_found}
+      {:error, output, code} ->
+        if code == 1 and String.contains?(output, "No such file") do
+          {:error, :not_found}
+        else
+          {:error, {:read_failed, "exit #{code}: #{String.slice(output, 0, 300)}"}}
+        end
     end

```

**Add bounded retry/backoff before the terminal PR-not-found fallback.**

When `find_open_pr` returns `{:error, :not_found}` immediately after builder completion, the code falls through to `BLOCKED.md` lookup without retrying. GitHub/`gh` PR visibility can lag by a few seconds, causing successful runs to immediately fail with "pr_not_found" even though the PR exists. Implement a short bounded retry (2-3 attempts with exponential backoff) before treating the miss as terminal.

Affects: lines 257-267 (and the detection flow starting at line 188)

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@conductor/lib/conductor/run_server.ex` around lines 188 - 192, The
PR-detection flow (detect_pr / find_open_pr) immediately treats {:error,
:not_found} as terminal and falls back to BLOCKED.md; add a bounded retry with
exponential backoff (e.g., 2–3 attempts with backoff 500ms -> 1s -> 2s) inside
detect_pr (or a small helper invoked by detect_pr) so that when find_open_pr
returns {:error, :not_found} you retry the lookup before calling the terminal
pr_not_found path and BLOCKED.md handling; ensure retries are limited, log each
retry (use log/2) and record a Store.record_event when finally giving up to
preserve existing telemetry.
```

**Add "no such file or directory" check for missing file detection.**

Line 396 only checks for `"not found"`, which matches `cat` command not found (a less common case), but misses the standard error `"No such file or directory"` that `cat` emits for missing files. This causes absent `BLOCKED.md` files to be classified as `workspace_read_error` instead of `:not_found`, breaking the error handling logic that relies on distinguishing between the two cases.

<details>
<summary>Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
-    if code == 1 and String.contains?(normalized_output, "not found") do
+    if code == 1 and
+         (String.contains?(normalized_output, "no such file") or
+            String.contains?(normalized_output, "not found")) do
```
</details>

```

classify_workspace_read_error function currently only checks for "not found" in
the normalized output, which misses the typical "no such file or directory"
message from cat; update classify_workspace_read_error to treat either case as a
not-found error by checking if String.contains?(normalized_output, "not found")
or String.contains?(normalized_output, "no such file or directory") and return
{:error, :not_found} when either matches, leaving the existing fallback that
returns {:error, %{output: ..., code: code}} unchanged.
```
